mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, confirming robust economic momentum that supports elevated equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and falling corporate output, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the regime-conditional alpha vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains above 4.0% while the federal funds rate stays below 5.0%, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes, causing immediate discount rate repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of threshold-crossing events, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.01 within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating a persistent upward drift in equity prices that materializes as positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of months, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to economic output, signaling an impending recessionary slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 1.5% and RSAFS MoM growth < 0.5%
failure_condition: The divergence condition produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and infrastructure investment, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of recovery-phase observations, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in walk-forward validation.
